title: dactylis glomerata subsp. lobata
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. lobata, also known as Cock's-foot or Loose Silky Bent, is a perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its distinctive hairy leaves and red anthers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it a popular treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. lobata include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
